task.title: Troubleshoot SQL performance after migration
task.shortdesc: Follow these steps to investigate performance degradation after moving a business system to a new SQL Server environment.

context.p: Use this procedure when users report slower performance after migration to a different server, storage platform, or network environment.

step.cmd: Confirm the scope of the issue.

info.p: Identify which transactions, reports, or queries are affected and whether all users see the same behaviour.

step.cmd: Compare the current environment with the previous environment.

info.p: Review differences in SQL Server version, storage type, protocol settings, permissions, and network path.
Verify whether the system is using Shared Memory, TCP/IP, or Named Pipes, and assess whether the selected protocol is appropriate for the deployment architecture.
Use available SQL monitoring tools to identify blocking, expensive queries, or plan regressions.
Check whether restricted permissions, views, or wrapper queries are adding unnecessary overhead.

step.cmd: Document findings and define corrective actions.

info.p: Record observations, probable causes, proposed mitigations, and any further testing required.

result.p: You have completed an initial structured assessment of the SQL performance issue and identified areas for further investigation or remediation.
